mnemonics: CCS: Cuts, Chandy-Lamport, Snapshots - A global state is a consistent cut across all processes, captured by the Chandy-Lamport algorithm to take a snapshot.
keyPoints: A global state is the set of local states of all processes and the state of all communication channels.

A consistent global state (or consistent cut) is one that could have actually occurred; it respects the 'happened-before' relation, meaning it does not include a message receipt without its corresponding send.
The Chandy-Lamport snapshot algorithm is a distributed algorithm used to record a consistent global state without freezing the entire system.
commonMistakes: Assuming any collection of local states forms a consistent global state, ignoring the state of the channels and the causality of messages.
Believing the Chandy-Lamport algorithm stops or significantly slows down the entire system; it is designed to be non-intrusive and allows processes to continue their execution.
